channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: A look at some of the worst fires at nightclubs and music venues
item.description: A fire at a nightclub in the town of Kocani in North Macedonia has killed 59 people and injured more than 150 others, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 59 dead and more than 150 injured in nightclub fire in North Macedonia
item.description: KOCANI, North Macedonia (AP) — A massive fire tore through a nightclub in North Macedonia ’s eastern town of Kocani early Sunday, killing 59 people and injuring 155, authorities said. The blaze broke out around 2:30 a.m.
item.dc:creator: BORIS GRDANOSKI and KONSTANTIN TESTORIDES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hamas says it will only release American-Israeli hostage if ceasefire deal is implemented
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Hamas said Saturday it will only release an American-Israeli and the bodies of four other hostages if Israel implements their ceasefire agreement, calling it an “exceptional deal” aimed at getting the truce back on track.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Russia and Ukraine trade overnight aerial attacks after Putin sets out conditions for ceasefire
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia and Ukraine traded heavy aerial blows overnight, with both sides on Saturday reporting more than 100 enemy drones over their respective territories. The attacks comes less than 24 hours after Russian President Vladimir Putin met with U.S.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US launches ‘decisive and powerful’ strikes against Houthis in Yemen
item.description: President Trump said the United States has launched “decisive and powerful” strikes against Houthi militants in Yemen, warning that the military “will use overwhelming lethal force” until the Iran-backed rebels stop their attacks on cargo ships and free up shipping lanes in the region.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Cuba still largely without power after nationwide grid collapse
item.description: Cuba remains largely without power a day after the country's grid collapsed, raising fresh questions about the island's generating system; officials gave no timeline for restoring service.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US strikes Houthis in Yemen as Trump warns Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump has ordered the United States military to "launch decisive and powerful" action against the Iran-backed Houthis in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Pope works on signature reform from hospital as Vatican cuts back on updates on medical progress
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis entered the fifth week of hospital treatment for double pneumonia Saturday very much looking ahead as he worked on a signature priority of his papacy and signs of his recovery remained on a positive trajectory.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Starmer challenges Putin to prove he is serious about peace by signing up to Ukraine ceasefire
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Saturday challenged Russian President Vladimir Putin to sign up to a ceasefire in its war against Ukraine if he is serious about peace, and said allies will keep increasing the pressure on the Kremlin, including by moving planning for a peacekeepi
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's pick for hostage negotiations envoy withdraws nomination but will continue on hostage issues
item.description: Adam Boehler, President Donald Trump’s nominee to become special envoy for hostage affairs, has withdrawn his nomination to avoid a requirement that he divest from his investment business.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Over 100,000 people join protest rally in Belgrade against Serbia's president and government
item.description: At least 100,000 people have converged in Belgrade for a massive rally in the Serbian capital seen as a culmination of months-long protests against populist President Aleksandar Vucic.
item.dc:creator: Jovana Gec And Dusan Stojanovic, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rubio declares South African ambassador to US 'persona non grata' over Trump comments
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio declared South African ambassador to the U.S. Ebrahim Rasool “persona non grata” and accused the diplomat of being a “race-baiting politician” over his recent comments about President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Thousands converging on Serbia's capital for a major rally that tests Vucic's populist government
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Tens of thousands of people swarmed the streets in Serbia's capital Belgrade on Friday, staging a joyful prelude to a major anti-government rally on the weekend and dealing a blow to populist President Aleksandar Vucic.
item.dc:creator: JOVANA GEC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US envoy is taking Putin's comments on Ukraine ceasefire proposal to Trump, Kremlin official says
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin met with U.S. envoy Steve Witkoff to discuss details of the American proposal for a 30-day ceasefire in the war with Ukraine, asking him to convey Moscow's thoughts to Washington, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hamas says it will release a US-Israeli hostage and 4 bodies, but Israel expresses immediate doubt
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Palestinian militant group Hamas said Friday that it has accepted a proposal from mediators to release one living American-Israeli hostage and the bodies of four dual nationals who had died in captivity.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Whipsawed by Trump's tariffs, the US public is getting a lot more nervous about the economy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's volatile tariff threats are unleashing historic jumps in public anxiety, with the potential to undermine his pledges to strengthen a U.S. economy that is increasingly weakened.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's Irish golf course damaged by pro-Palestinian activists
item.description: The incident comes less than a week after pro-Palestinian protestors dug up some of the Trump Turnberry greens in Scotland.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas says it will release American hostage, agrees to restart Gaza ceasefire talks
item.description: Hamas said Friday that it agreed to release American Israeli hostage Edan Alexander and the bodies of four other Americans, following a proposal from mediators to resume negotiations with Israel over a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Zelenskyy accuses Putin of trying to stall ceasefire talks, pushes Trump for tougher sanctions
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is pushing President Donald Trump to impose sanctions on Russia, as he believes Russian President Vladimir Putin is trying to prolong the war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Johns Hopkins University says it is cutting more than 2,000 workers due to cut to federal aid
item.description: Johns Hopkins University said Thursday that it is cutting over 2,000 workers because of a federal funding cut. “The termination of more than $800 million in [U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: UN official denies seeing 'a shred of evidence' showing staff in Gaza held hostages
item.description: Tom Fletcher, a top UN humanitarian aid official, says he has not seen "a shred of evidence" linking the international body's workers in Gaza to Hamas hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump 'hopes' Putin agrees to ceasefire as Moscow signals no truce yet
item.description: President Donald Trump said he "hopes" Russia agrees to a ceasefire deal after Ukraine accepted the terms earlier this week and warned it would be a "very disappointing moment for the world" otherwise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Poland calls on US to place nukes within its borders amid Russia threat
item.description: Polish President Duda has once again called on the US to place its nuclear arms within its borders to better deter Russia, mirroring talks with France amid security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump meets with NATO chief Mark Rutte
item.description: The meeting comes after Trump called on European allies to contribute more to the alliance's defense fund.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel kills 8 Palestinians in Gaza as Hamas says ceasefire talks resume
item.description: Israeli fire has killed eight Palestinians in the Gaza Strip and three more in the occupied West Bank over the past 24 hours, Palestinian officials said Tuesday, even as a fragile ceasefire with Hamas in Gaza has largely held since late January.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Search for Sudiksha Konanki, missing Pitt student, continues in Dominican Republic
item.description: Sudiksha Konanki was last seen early Thursday morning on a beach near Punta Cana. Police have spoken with a man allegedly with her at the time.
item.dc:creator: Nick Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Pope gets good news from doctors: He's no longer in immediate danger
item.description: Doctors are still cautious and have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Canada's conservatives trying to associate Carney with Trudeau
item.description: Conservatives are on the defense after the Liberal Party picked Carney as Canada's next leader, a journalist says.
item.dc:creator: Connell McShane, NewsNation

item.title: Trump labels ex-Australian PM Malcolm Turnbull 'weak' amid spat
item.description: The president's attack on Turnbull came after comments Turnbull made to Bloomberg that Donald Trump's style is an "advantage" to China.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Doctors declare Pope no longer in imminent danger, but remains hospitalized
item.description: Doctors said Monday that Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Syria's worst violence in months reopens wounds of the civil war
item.description: An ambush on a Syrian security patrol by gunmen loyal to ousted leader Bashar Assad escalated into clashes that a war monitor estimates have killed more than 1,000 people over four days.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-central banker to replace Trudeau as Canada's prime minister after winning Liberal Party vote
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Former central banker Mark Carney will become Canada’s next prime minister after the governing Liberal Party elected him its leader Sunday as the country deals with U.S. President Donald Trump’s trade war and annexation threat, and a federal election looms.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Canada braces for new leader: Here are Trudeau’s potential successors
item.description: Canada is bracing for Prime Minister Justin Trudeau’s successor, who will follow the Liberal Party leader that has led U.S.’s neighbor to the north since the Obama administration, The Associated Press reported. The change in leadership will come amid frosty U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation
